title: The One With Barry And Mindys Wedding

jokes.text: After the scene... Mr. Beatty comes up to me and he says, 'Good actor. Bad kisser.'
jokes.setup: Joey is explaining a problem from his audition with Warren Beatty
jokes.punchline: The twist that the legendary lover Warren Beatty called Joey a bad kisser
jokes.explanation: Strong irony - Warren Beatty, famous for being a legendary lover, calling someone a bad kisser. Well-structured reveal and perfectly fits Joey's character arc.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Warren Beatty said I was a bad kisser!

jokes.text: That's like... Mother Teresa, not a good mother. Pfft.
jokes.setup: Joey trying to find an analogy for how absurd it is that he's called a bad kisser
jokes.punchline: The completely wrong analogy comparing kissing to Mother Teresa's mothering
jokes.explanation: Shows Joey's confused logic - the analogy doesn't quite work but that's the joke. Moderately funny character moment.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: It's like calling Mother Teresa a bad mother!

jokes.text: What does Warren Beatty know about kissing? Heh.
jokes.setup: Ross trying to comfort Joey by dismissing Warren Beatty's opinion
jokes.punchline: The obvious irony that Warren Beatty is famously known for his romantic prowess
jokes.explanation: Basic irony joke - Ross's immediate realization makes it work but it's fairly predictable.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Look, who cares what Warren Beatty thinks? He's just some guy.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Just some guy? Ross, Warren Beatty is like, the most famous lover in Hollywood!

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: So? That doesn't mean his opinion matters.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Are you kidding me? Warren Beatty's romantic reputation is legendary!

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Well... okay, maybe his opinion matters a little.

jokes.text: Oh. Heh, heh.
jokes.setup: Ross realizes he just said something stupid about Warren Beatty not knowing about kissing
jokes.punchline: His awkward realization and nervous laugh
jokes.explanation: Simple reaction beat - the laugh track moment when someone realizes their mistake.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Wait, wait, wait. Did I just say that Warren Beatty doesn't know how to kiss? *nervous laugh* Oh God, oh God, oh God.

jokes.text: Forget it. - Yeah, right.
jokes.setup: Joey asks one of the girls to kiss him for practice
jokes.punchline: Both immediately refusing
jokes.explanation: Quick, efficient reaction - shows the girls' shared disgust at the idea.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Hey, can one of you kiss me? I need to practice for an audition.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: No.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Absolutely not.

jokes.text: I'll do it. I've kissed him before. I can do it again.
jokes.setup: After Monica and Rachel refuse, someone volunteers
jokes.punchline: Rachel's resigned, practical attitude about kissing Joey again
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Rachel's matter-of-fact approach to helping a friend, with history providing context.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: I'll do it.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: No, Joey, that's okay. We already kissed.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: I know, but I don't mind doing it again.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Yeah, well, I do. I'm not kissing you again, Joey.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Why not? What's the big deal?

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Because the last time we kissed, it was weird.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Weird? I thought it was great!

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Yeah, well, it was weird for me. So no, I'm not doing it again.

jokes.text: Good. Very good. Firm, but tender. I'd recommend you to a friend.
jokes.setup: Rachel giving Joey feedback on his kissing after their practice session
jokes.punchline: Treating the kiss like a professional service review
jokes.explanation: Clever line treating intimate kissing like a Yelp review. Good economy and unexpected approach.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Okay, so... you're very enthusiastic, which is great. But maybe you could be a little more gentle? You know, like you're not trying to suck out my soul.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Gentle. Got it. Anything else?

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Well, your technique is solid, but you tend to lead with your tongue a little too much. It's like... five stars for effort, two stars for execution.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Two stars? What do I have to do to get five?

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Practice. And maybe... don't think about it so much? You're kissing someone, not performing open-heart surgery.

jokes.text: Over my dead body.
jokes.setup: Someone suggests Chandler should practice kissing men
jokes.punchline: Monica's immediate, emphatic refusal
jokes.explanation: Standard protective girlfriend reaction - functional but predictable.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: No! No way! I'm not doing that!

jokes.text: And I'll be using his dead body as a shield.
jokes.setup: Monica said 'over my dead body' about Chandler kissing men
jokes.punchline: Chandler's darkly comic escalation of the death imagery
jokes.explanation: Classic Chandler - takes Monica's expression literally and escalates it absurdly. Perfect character voice and timing.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Well, you say that now, but when the time comes, I think you'll be very relaxed about it.

jokes.text: looking like something you drink when you're nauseous.
jokes.setup: Rachel complaining about having to walk down the aisle in her bridesmaid dress
jokes.punchline: Comparing her appearance to Pepto-Bismol or similar medicine
jokes.explanation: Vivid, relatable description of an ugly bridesmaid dress. Good visual comedy through dialogue.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: I have to walk down the aisle in this thing? I look like a Pepto-Bismol bottle!

jokes.text: Yeah, well, you promised Barry you'd marry him.
jokes.setup: Rachel says she promised Mindy she'd be maid of honor
jokes.punchline: Monica pointing out Rachel's track record with promises to Barry
jokes.explanation: Sharp callback that undercuts Rachel's reasoning. Monica being the practical voice of reason.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Wait, didn't you promise Barry you'd marry him?

jokes.text: Someone's wearing the same clothes they had on last night. Get a little action?
jokes.setup: Joey notices Chandler in yesterday's clothes
jokes.punchline: Immediately assuming it means sexual activity
jokes.explanation: Standard Joey line - his mind goes straight to sex. Fits character but predictable.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Whoa, whoa, whoa! Walk of shame!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: What? No, I just fell asleep at Monica's.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Sure you did. In those clothes? From yesterday?

jokes.text: Whoo-hoo! Stud! ... Whoo-hoo! Geek!
jokes.setup: Joey celebrates Chandler getting action, then learns it was just talking on the internet
jokes.punchline: Immediate reversal from 'Stud!' to 'Geek!'
jokes.explanation: Nice comedic reversal - Joey's enthusiasm completely flipping based on new information. Good structure.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Stud!

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Geek!

jokes.text: You know how I tend to get a little defended and quippy? - Get out. - No. Please.
jokes.setup: Chandler asking if they know he gets defensive and quippy
jokes.punchline: Joey's immediate sarcastic response proving the point
jokes.explanation: Excellent meta-joke where the show acknowledges Chandler's character traits, and Joey immediately demonstrates them. Perfect character integration.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: You guys know I get defensive and quippy when I'm uncomfortable, right?

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Oh, really? Because I had no idea. This is all news to me.

jokes.text: Stop it. You're freaking me out. ... Yeah. I don't like you this way.
jokes.setup: Chandler being sincere instead of his usual quippy self
jokes.punchline: The friends being disturbed by Chandler acting genuinely
jokes.explanation: Good character humor - the group preferring Chandler's defense mechanisms to genuine emotion.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: You know, I've been thinking about our friendship, and I just want to say that you guys mean the world to me. I don't say it enough, but I really do love you all.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Oh my God, are you okay? Do you have a tumor?

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Is this some kind of joke? Because it's not funny.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Chandler, you're scaring me.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Yeah man, just go back to making fun of us or something.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: This is weird. I don't like it.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: What? I can't just express my feelings?

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: No. No, you can't. It's not natural.

jokes.text: I think my boyfriend's ever so dreamy.
jokes.setup: Monica and Richard being overly romantic in public
jokes.punchline: Monica's exaggerated, saccharine language
jokes.explanation: Mild cringe humor from overly cutesy couple talk. Sets up the awkwardness but not particularly clever.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Oh Richard, you're like a warm, buttery croissant on a cold winter morning, filling my soul with the golden essence of your love.

dialogue.speaker: Richard
dialogue.line: Monica, that's... very poetic.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: And you're like a strong, dark espresso that awakens my deepest desires, a cappuccino of passion that—

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Okay, we get it. You two are in love. Can we please just order our coffee now?

jokes.text: Afraid to ask him? - Could not be more terrified.
jokes.setup: Ross asking if Monica's afraid to ask Richard about marriage
jokes.punchline: Monica's hyperbolic honesty about her terror
jokes.explanation: Good contrast between the careful question and Monica's blunt, vulnerable response.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Are you afraid to ask him about marriage?

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Afraid? Ross, I'm not afraid. I'm terrified. I'm so scared that when I think about talking to him, I get physically ill. I can't eat, I can't sleep, I just lie there at night, sweating through my sheets, imagining all the ways he could say no.

jokes.text: Give Rachel another chance to dress like Princess Bubble Yum.
jokes.setup: Ross suggesting Monica should consider marriage
jokes.punchline: Referencing Rachel's awful bridesmaid dress from earlier
jokes.explanation: Good callback that enhances the visual gag from earlier. 'Princess Bubble Yum' is a vivid description.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: You should really think about getting married, Mon.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Why would I do that?

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Well, at least you'd only have to wear one awful bridesmaid dress instead of multiple ones like Rachel does.

jokes.text: That's probably why babies learn to talk, so they can tell grownups to cut it out.
jokes.setup: Adults playing peek-a-boo with a baby
jokes.punchline: Chandler's theory about babies' motivation to speak
jokes.explanation: Classic Chandler observational humor with a fresh take on baby development. Original and perfectly in character.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: You know what I think? I think babies don't actually want to talk. They're just playing peek-a-boo with language. Like, they know the word, but they're not gonna say it because they think it's funny to watch us freak out.

jokes.text: Like a hound?
jokes.setup: Monica asking if Richard sees a bassinet in their future
jokes.punchline: Richard mishearing 'bassinet' as 'basset' (hound)
jokes.explanation: Simple wordplay joke. Efficient but not particularly clever - relies on similar-sounding words.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Do you see a bassinet in our future?

dialogue.speaker: Richard
dialogue.line: A basset? Monica, I'm 61 years old. I don't have the energy for a basset hound.

jokes.text: Well, I just think the baby would keep falling off the dog.
jokes.setup: Richard realizing Monica meant bassinet not basset hound
jokes.punchline: His matter-of-fact explanation of why a dog wouldn't work
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan delivery of absurd logic. Richard playing it completely straight makes it funnier.

dialogue.speaker: Richard
dialogue.line: A dog wouldn't work. It would chew up the baby's toys, shed all over everything, and you'd have to walk it at three in the morning when the baby's crying anyway.

jokes.text: I'm talking hovercrafts and apes taking over the planet.
jokes.setup: Monica trying to downplay how far in the future she means about having kids
jokes.punchline: Escalating to science fiction scenarios
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Monica's nervousness through increasingly ridiculous timeframes.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: I want to have kids... eventually.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Like, maybe in five or ten years.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Or twenty years.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Okay, fifty years.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: When I'm ninety-seven and they've invented that thing where they can just grow babies in tubes.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: And also time travel, so I can go back and meet them as children.

jokes.text: I'm sorry, we don't have your sheep.
jokes.setup: Someone entering the apartment
jokes.punchline: Joey's random response to what was presumably a normal greeting
jokes.explanation: Random Joey non-sequitur. Fits his character but relies on randomness for humor.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: How you doin'?

jokes.text: You, I could eat you with a spoon.
jokes.setup: Ross complimenting Rachel despite her awful dress
jokes.punchline: The overly cutesy, infantilizing compliment
jokes.explanation: Ross being overly romantic in a slightly creepy way. Mildly funny character moment.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: That dress is so... pretty! It's like something a little girl would wear to her first tea party with her dolly!

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Ross...

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: No, no, I mean it! Very... youthful. Like you just learned what clothes are!

jokes.text: She likes art... and I like funny words.
jokes.setup: Chandler explaining why he's looking at the Guggenheim website with his internet girlfriend
jokes.punchline: His honest admission that he's just there for the humor
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chandler line - self-aware about his lack of culture while being endearingly honest. Great economy.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Well, I'm looking at the Guggenheim website because my internet girlfriend thinks it's impressive. But honestly, I'm just there for the humor.

jokes.text: Are you the cutest? - I'm afraid I might just be.
jokes.setup: Rachel teasing Chandler about being cute with his online relationship
jokes.punchline: Chandler's mock-modest admission
jokes.explanation: Chandler's characteristic false modesty. Solid character moment but not exceptional.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Oh my God, you're being so cute about this!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: I know, I'm adorable. Could I BE any more adorable?

jokes.text: It could be like a big, giant guy.
jokes.setup: After suggesting Chandler's online girlfriend could be a guy
jokes.punchline: Joey escalating to emphasize just how big and masculine this hypothetical guy could be
jokes.explanation: Joey's mind going to extremes. Fits his character but fairly predictable escalation.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Could be a guy! A big guy! A really big guy with a beard! Who works at a lumberyard!

jokes.text: How do you not fall down more?
jokes.setup: Ross suggesting to ask about life expectancy to determine if she's a woman
jokes.punchline: Chandler's incredulous response to Ross's logic
jokes.explanation: Great Chandler line - perfect mix of sarcasm and genuine bewilderment at Ross's stupidity. Excellent economy.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: So you're gonna ask her how long she's gonna live?

jokes.text: That's why you never see pigeons in sushi bars.
jokes.setup: Phoebe explaining why you shouldn't throw rice at weddings because it kills pigeons
jokes.punchline: Her completely illogical conclusion about sushi bars
jokes.explanation: Classic Phoebe logic - starts with a real fact then veers into absurd territory. Perfect character integration.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: You can't throw rice at weddings anymore because the birds eat it and it kills them. So now people throw birdseed, which is stupid because the birds are just gonna eat that too. They're gonna be like, 'Oh boy, birdseed!' It's a death trap!

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: And that's why you should never eat sushi. Because the rice is a death trap for the fish.

jokes.text: in the bathroom, I saw the window I crawled out of at my wedding.
jokes.setup: Rachel explaining why she's having second thoughts about the wedding
jokes.punchline: The casual reference to her dramatic wedding escape
jokes.explanation: Good callback to the pilot. Rachel's matter-of-fact delivery of something so dramatic works well.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: I just... I don't know. Every time I think about walking down that aisle, I keep remembering the last time I did that. And I literally ran away in my wedding dress.

jokes.text: Why didn't you tell me? - What could I do? Stand up and shout, 'Hey, Rachel, your butt is showing!'
jokes.setup: Rachel discovering her dress mishap
jokes.punchline: Ross explaining the awkwardness of the situation he was in
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the visual gag setup. Ross's predicament is relatable and funny.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Oh my God, Ross! You could have told me my dress was inside out!

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: I tried! I tried to tell you, but every time I opened my mouth, Chandler kept making faces at me, and Monica was giving me this look like I was about to say something inappropriate, and Joey just kept talking about food!

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: So what? You just let me walk around like that?

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: I was in an impossible situation! If I told you, it would be weird. If I didn't tell you, it was also weird. There was no winning here, Rachel!

jokes.text: when I was in eighth grade... and I had to sing the 'Copacabana' in front of the entire school. Two lines into it, I ran and freaked out.
jokes.setup: Rachel comparing her current humiliation to past embarrassments
jokes.punchline: The specific detail of running away during Copacabana
jokes.explanation: Sets up the climactic Copacabana callback. Good character backstory that feels authentic.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: I've been humiliated before, but this is the worst. Remember when I tripped at the Copacabana and everyone saw me fall? I just ran out of there. I didn't even wait for my drink.

jokes.text: Ross, would you stop! You got me. I'm dating you.
jokes.setup: Ross trying to comfort Rachel about her past embarrassment
jokes.punchline: Rachel's exasperated acknowledgment that she's already committed to him
jokes.explanation: Rachel's practical way of telling Ross to stop trying so hard. Good relationship humor.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Hey, it's okay. Everyone does embarrassing things. It doesn't define who you are.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Ross, I know that. But he saw me! He literally saw me!

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: And? So what? He probably forgot about it already.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: How can you be so sure?

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Because people are generally self-absorbed. Trust me.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Well, it's too late now anyway. I'm already committed to him.

jokes.text: In fact, I hardly expected to see so much.
jokes.setup: Mr. Weinberg commenting on seeing Rachel again
jokes.punchline: The understated reference to seeing her exposed
jokes.explanation: Polite elderly man's way of referencing the wardrobe malfunction. Good restraint in the delivery.

dialogue.speaker: Mr. Weinberg
dialogue.line: It's been a long time since I've seen Rachel like that.

jokes.text: You told me you didn't see anything. - I tell you a lot of things.
jokes.setup: Rachel confronting Ross about lying
jokes.punchline: Ross's admission that he lies to her regularly
jokes.explanation: Ross accidentally revealing more than intended. Good relationship humor with excellent economy.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: You lie to me?

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: No! Well... yes. But only about important things.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Like what?

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Like... how much I care about you. I tell you I care about you this much, but I actually care about you this much.

jokes.text: I'm Mrs. Dr. Barry Hunter, hyphen, Farber.
jokes.setup: Mindy introducing herself as a married woman
jokes.punchline: The ridiculously long hyphenated name
jokes.explanation: Good observation about modern marriage naming complications. Feels authentic.

dialogue.speaker: Mindy
dialogue.line: Hi, I'm Mindy Feldman-Henderson-Rothschild-Grayson-Carmichael-DuPont-Vanderbilt-Astor-Whitney-Rockefeller.

jokes.text: At our wedding, they were packing up the chopped liver about now.
jokes.setup: Barry commenting on how their wedding is going compared to his previous one
jokes.punchline: The matter-of-fact reference to how quickly Rachel's wedding ended
jokes.explanation: Barry's surprisingly good-natured callback to his own humiliation. Well-delivered.

dialogue.speaker: Barry
dialogue.line: Well, this wedding is going much better than my first one.

dialogue.speaker: Barry
dialogue.line: That one only lasted three minutes.

jokes.text: After you ran out on your wedding... Barry's parents told people you were sort of... insane. - Insane? - From the syphilis.
jokes.setup: Explaining why people keep asking about Rachel's health
jokes.punchline: The escalation from 'insane' to 'from syphilis'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation and absurd explanation for Rachel's behavior. The specificity of syphilis makes it funnier.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Everyone keeps asking if I'm okay because I look sick.

dialogue.speaker: Mindy
dialogue.line: Well, first they think you're just insane.

dialogue.speaker: Barry
dialogue.line: Then they assume it's from syphilis.

jokes.text: What were they gonna say? You didn't love me anymore? Come on.
jokes.setup: Rachel asking why they said she had syphilis
jokes.punchline: Barry's complete inability to accept the truth about why she left
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing Barry's narcissism. He literally finds syphilis more believable than being unloved.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Why would you tell people I have syphilis?

dialogue.speaker: Barry
dialogue.line: I was just trying to help you save face. I thought it would be better than telling people the truth.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Better than what truth?

dialogue.speaker: Barry
dialogue.line: That you left me at the altar! But I figured nobody had to know that, so I told them you were sick.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: You told people I had syphilis instead of telling them I left you?

dialogue.speaker: Barry
dialogue.line: Yes! Because if people knew you left me, they might think there was something wrong with me. This way, it's just you with a disease.

jokes.text: She wants to know what's wrong. - What's wrong? You're married! That's what's wrong!
jokes.setup: Phoebe asking why Chandler won't respond to his internet girlfriend
jokes.punchline: Chandler's exasperated statement of the obvious problem
jokes.explanation: Standard reaction to Phoebe missing the obvious. Functional but predictable.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Why won't you respond to her?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Because she's not real, Phoebe. She's on the internet.

jokes.text: What if the husband is the wrong guy and you are the right guy?
jokes.setup: Phoebe trying to convince Chandler to meet his internet girlfriend despite her being married
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's romantic, logic-defying worldview
jokes.explanation: Classic Phoebe romantic idealism overriding practical concerns. Good character consistency.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: So what if she's married? That doesn't mean anything. My third husband was married when I met him.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Your third husband was married to you!

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Exactly! So I know it can work.

jokes.text: If you don't meet her, you'll kick yourself when you're 80... which is hard to do and that's how you break a hip.
jokes.setup: Phoebe warning Chandler about future regret
jokes.punchline: The practical concern about elderly flexibility
jokes.explanation: Classic Phoebe - starts philosophical then veers into practical medical concerns. Great structure and character voice.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: You're gonna regret not being flexible when you're older.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: I'm gonna regret a lot of things when I'm older. Not being able to lick my own butt is not gonna be high on that list.

jokes.text: No, wait! You have to take a shower because ew! And I'll get the... No, you have to answer her. Answer her first. No, make some coffee because it's too much.
jokes.setup: Phoebe getting overwhelmed trying to help Chandler prepare
jokes.punchline: Her complete breakdown into contradictory instructions
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy showing Phoebe's anxiety manifesting as helpful chaos. Classic character moment.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Okay, okay, I can help you with this. Just... just stay calm and listen to me carefully.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: No, wait, do it this way. Actually, no, the other way was better. Wait, what were we doing?

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: You need to do it faster! But also slower, so you don't mess up. But hurry!

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Don't think about it, just do it! But think about it carefully first!

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: I'm helping! I'm being so helpful right now!

jokes.text: Tall and tan, and young and lovely... Okay, one more, please. I'm gonna get it in this time.
jokes.setup: Rachel attempting to sing Copacabana again
jokes.punchline: Her determination to overcome her childhood trauma
jokes.explanation: Good setup for the climactic moment. Rachel facing her fears in a small way first.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: I'm going to sing Copacabana again. I don't care what happened the last time.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: I have to face my fears. This is about overcoming my childhood trauma.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Rachel, your childhood trauma was that your parents wouldn't buy you a pony.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Exactly! And now I'm going to conquer this song!

jokes.text: But I want her so badly... I do. I have to have children. I'm sorry, I just do.
jokes.setup: Monica trying to convince herself she doesn't need children
jokes.punchline: Her complete breakdown and admission of her true feelings
jokes.explanation: Strong character moment. Monica's inability to lie to herself about what she wants. More touching than funny but works.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: I don't need children. I'm perfectly happy without them. I have my career, my apartment, my friends...

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Who am I kidding? I want a baby! I want to have a baby so badly! Why can't I have a baby?!

jokes.text: What? You hired the same band. I can't use the same speech?
jokes.setup: Best man getting called out for reusing his speech from Rachel and Barry's wedding
jokes.punchline: His matter-of-fact justification for the recycling
jokes.explanation: Clever meta-humor about wedding speech recycling. Good economy and realistic character logic.

dialogue.speaker: Best Man
dialogue.line: What? It worked the first time!

jokes.text: Not a lot of women would have the guts to come back here. And even fewer who'd do it with their asses hanging out.
jokes.setup: Best man praising Rachel's courage in his speech
jokes.punchline: The turn to crude humor about her wardrobe malfunction
jokes.explanation: Good structure - starts as genuine compliment then turns into roast. Effective but somewhat crude.

dialogue.speaker: Best Man
dialogue.line: Rachel showed real courage today. She walked down that aisle with such confidence and grace.

dialogue.speaker: Best Man
dialogue.line: Of course, that confidence lasted until we all saw what was holding up her dress.

jokes.text: she did not run out on Barry because she had syphilis.
jokes.setup: Ross trying to defend Rachel in his impromptu speech
jokes.punchline: Making the syphilis rumor worse by addressing it publicly
jokes.explanation: Classic Ross move - trying to help but making things worse. Good escalation of the syphilis joke.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: And another thing! The rumor about Rachel and syphilis is completely false!

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: She does not have syphilis. She never had syphilis. And frankly, I'm insulted that anyone would even suggest—

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: You know what? Now everyone's going to think she has syphilis!

jokes.text: Which, incidentally, worked out pretty well for me.
jokes.setup: Ross explaining that Rachel left Barry because she didn't love him
jokes.punchline: His understated acknowledgment of his own benefit
jokes.explanation: Good Ross moment - awkwardly honest about benefiting from Barry's loss. Nice economy.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: She didn't love him.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Well, I mean, that's good for me.

jokes.text: And once again, she is out of here! Okay, who had 9:45?
jokes.setup: Rachel leaving another Barry event dramatically
jokes.punchline: Someone apparently betting on when she'd leave
jokes.explanation: Great escalation showing Rachel's reputation has become a betting opportunity. Clever and well-structured.

dialogue.speaker: Wedding Guest
dialogue.line: That's the third Barry event Rachel's walked out of this month.

dialogue.speaker: Wedding Guest
dialogue.line: I've got twenty bucks says she makes it through the rehearsal dinner next week.

dialogue.speaker: Barry
dialogue.line: You're on. I give her ten minutes.

jokes.text: I promised myself I'd make it through at least one of your weddings.
jokes.setup: Rachel deciding not to leave the wedding
jokes.punchline: Her implication that Barry will have multiple weddings
jokes.explanation: Excellent burn delivered matter-of-factly. Implies Barry's marriages don't last. Great economy and character voice.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: I can't leave the wedding. Barry's going to have so many more of these.

jokes.text: Her name was Lola, She was a showgirl... With yellow feathers in her hair And a dress cut down to there...
jokes.setup: Rachel finally conquering her Copacabana fear by performing it
jokes.punchline: Her successful completion of the song that traumatized her
jokes.explanation: Perfect character payoff. Rachel overcoming her fear in the most public, dramatic way possible. Excellent structure and emotional satisfaction.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: I did it. I actually did it. I performed the Copacabana and I didn't have a panic attack.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Oh my God, Rach, that was amazing!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: You were incredible. I didn't know you had that in you.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Neither did I. Who knew all I needed was to face my fears head-on?

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: That's what I've been telling people for years.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Your aura is like, totally different now. It's all sparkly and trauma-free.

jokes.text: Copa, Copacabana, The hottest spot north of Havana... At the Copa, Copacabana, Music and passion were always In fashion at the Copa
jokes.setup: Rachel successfully performing Copacabana
jokes.punchline: Everyone joining in for a full musical number
jokes.explanation: Big crowd-pleasing moment. More about emotional payoff than pure comedy, but the escalation works.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: At the Copa... she lost her love.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: His name was Rico.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: He wore a diamond.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: He was escaping to New York, La-La-La-La-La.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: At the Copa... she lost her love.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: His name was Rico.

dialogue.speaker: All
dialogue.line: At the Copa!

jokes.text: If kids is what it takes to be with you, then kids it is.
jokes.setup: Richard making a big romantic gesture
jokes.punchline: His slightly unromantic phrasing of the sacrifice
jokes.explanation: Richard trying to be romantic but revealing his reluctance. Mild relationship humor.

dialogue.speaker: Richard
dialogue.line: I'm willing to give up my entire record collection for you.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Really? That's so sweet!

dialogue.speaker: Richard
dialogue.line: Well, I made copies.

jokes.text: And if you hadn't have said... 'If I have to,' like, 17 times... then I'd be saying, 'Okay, let's do it.'
jokes.setup: Monica responding to Richard's gesture
jokes.punchline: Calling out his obvious reluctance and repetitive phrasing
jokes.explanation: Monica being perceptive about Richard's true feelings. Good relationship comedy with specific observation.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: What? You're doing it again! You keep saying 'I know this is crazy' — if you know it's crazy, why are you doing it?

dialogue.speaker: Richard
dialogue.line: Because I love you.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Then just say that! Don't lead with the disclaimer!

jokes.text: Yeah, because life's just that kind.
jokes.setup: Chandler thinking a woman might be his internet girlfriend
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's sarcastic take on life's tendency toward cruel irony
jokes.explanation: Classic Phoebe wisdom disguised as sarcasm. Great economy and character voice.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Oh, that's nice. You know what they say - life is full of little surprises, and most of them are terrible.

jokes.text: like a watched pot. If you keep looking at it, the door's never gonna boil.
jokes.setup: Joey trying to use the 'watched pot never boils' saying
jokes.punchline: Confusing the metaphor by mixing pot and door
jokes.explanation: Classic Joey malapropism. Mixing metaphors in a way that's perfectly in character.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: A watched pot never boils, right? So I'm not gonna watch the pot.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: But I am gonna watch the door, because a watched door never... opens?

jokes.text: Oh... my... God.
jokes.setup: Chandler seeing who his internet girlfriend really is
jokes.punchline: The revelation that it's Janice
jokes.explanation: Perfect sitcom twist. Sets up the entire internet romance storyline to pay off with the worst possible person for Chandler. Excellent structure and character integration.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Oh. My. God.

dialogue.speaker: Janice
dialogue.line: Hi Chandler!

jokes.text: I've been feeling incredibly guilty because I wanna be a good friend. And damn it, I am a good friend. So shut up and close your eyes.
jokes.setup: Monica deciding to help Joey with his kissing problem
jokes.punchline: Her aggressive way of being helpful
jokes.explanation: Good Monica character moment - her competitiveness extending to being the best friend. Nice escalation.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Okay, I'm going to help you with your kissing.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Really?

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Yeah, come here.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Okay, the problem is you're too stiff. You need to relax your face.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Okay.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: And stop doing that thing with your lips.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: What thing?

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: That thing! Where you do... that.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Here, let me show you.

jokes.text: You are a good friend. The audition was this morning, and I didn't get it. But that was a hell of a kiss. Rachel is a very lucky girl.
jokes.setup: Joey thanking Monica for helping with his kissing
jokes.punchline: The revelation that the audition already happened and the compliment to Ross
jokes.explanation: Nice button to the kissing storyline. Joey being gracious in defeat and complimentary to Ross.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Thank you so much for helping me with my kissing technique, Monica. I really feel like I nailed it.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: That's great, Joey! When's your audition?

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Oh, it was this morning.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: This morning?! Joey, why didn't you tell me?

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: I know, I know. But hey, I learned something valuable today.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: What's that?

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Ross is a really good kisser.

tmdb_overview: Rachel agrees to be maid of honor at her ex-fiancé's wedding; she relives the nightmare of her own failed wedding day. Meanwhile, Chandler becomes enamored with a woman he meets on the Internet, but is surprised when he finally meets her. Monica ponders her future with Richard.
insight: Wedding episode misfires: 64.8 score masks uneven pacing across scattered character threads.
